Project Helios — Q3 Roadmap
INTERNAL DRAFT — Not for client distribution
Last updated: 2026-07-06

Q3 2026 Priorities:

1. COMPLETE SYSTEM INTEGRATION (July)
   - Finalize data ingestion module (target: 2026-07-18)
   - Complete client dashboard v1 (target: 2026-07-22)
   - Integration test sign-off (target: 2026-07-25)

2. PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (July 25)
   - Live data ingestion demonstration using Meridian sample data
   - End-to-end pipeline walkthrough
   - Dashboard visualization with real data (per contract Section 9.3)

3. USER ACCEPTANCE TESTING — MERIDIAN GROUP (August 17-28)
   - Deploy to UAT environment
   - Support Meridian user testing sessions
   - Address UAT feedback (budget: 2 sprints)

4. PERFORMANCE HARDENING (August-September)
   - Dashboard load time target: <2 seconds for 100k records
   - Ingestion throughput benchmarking (target: 500k records/min sustained)
   - External security penetration test (vendor TBD)

5. LAUNCH PREPARATION (September)
   - Production environment setup and runbook documentation
   - Hypercare plan finalization (30-day post-launch model)
   - Final client training sessions

Target launch date: 2026-10-01
Key risk: UAT slippage of >2 weeks pushes launch into Q4, triggering Section 7.1 penalty review.

Proposed Q3 feature additions (subject to change control approval):
- Scheduled ingestion jobs (cron-based triggers)
- Email alerting for pipeline failures
- Self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

Week of 2026-07-06

Overall: ON TRACK (minor risks)

Milestone Progress:
- Data ingestion module: 85% complete
- API gateway: 100% complete (delivered)
- Client dashboard: 60% complete
- Integration testing: 40% complete

Sprint 14 Goals:

   Dev team awaiting clarification from Meridian IT. Estimated delay: 2-3 days.

   Target resolution: 2026-07-14.

Next milestone: Integration Test Sign-off — target 2026-07-25

           Priya Kapoor (Meridian IT Lead), James Whitfield (Meridian Project Sponsor)

3. UAT period extended by 1 week (now Aug 17-28)

Action Items:
- NovaBridge: Resolve GeoSync license before demo (owner: Derek Okafor, due 2026-07-14)
- NovaBridge: Send draft demo agenda to Meridian by 2026-07-18 (owner: Sarah Chen)
- Meridian: Share IT infrastructure diagram for integration environment setup
  (owner: Priya Kapoor, due 2026-07-12)

Notes:
- James Whitfield emphasized that the demo must include live data ingestion, not mock data.
- Concern raised about dashboard load times on large datasets. Derek to benchmark before demo.
- Priya Kapoor confirmed Meridian IT will provision UAT environment access by 2026-07-19.

Project Helios — Risk Register
Last reviewed: 2026-07-07 | Owner: Sarah Chen (PM)

ID    Risk Description                             Likelihood  Impact  Score  Mitigation Owner    Status
----  -------------------------------------------  ----------  ------  -----  ------------------  --------
R-01  Meridian source data quality issues           High        High    9      Amir Hassan         Active — date format blocker confirmed in sprint 14
R-02  GeoSync license not renewed before demo       Medium      High    6      Raj Patel           Active — legal review in progress, due 2026-07-14

R-05  Key person dependency: Derek Okafor           Low         High    3      Sarah Chen          Monitored — no backup tech lead identified
R-06  Meridian IT environment access delays        Medium      Medium  4      Nadia Petrov        Active — awaiting IT infrastructure diagram
R-07  Scope creep from Meridian feature requests   Low         Medium  2      Raj Patel           Monitored — 3 informal requests logged, not yet escalated
R-08  Cloud cost overrun                           Low         Low     1      Nadia Petrov        Monitored
R-09  Third-party API deprecation (non-GeoSync)    Very Low    Medium  1      Olivia Brooks       Monitored

Project Helios — Architecture Overview

System Components:

1. Data Ingestion Layer
   Accepts source data via REST API or SFTP. Handles CSV, JSON, and XML formats.
   Supports batch and streaming modes. Built-in format validation and schema enforcement.

2. Transformation Engine
   Applies client-defined mapping rules. Built on Apache Spark.
   Processes up to 500k records/minute. Configurable via YAML rule files.

3. API Gateway
   RESTful interface for downstream consumers. Auth via OAuth 2.0.
   Rate-limited at 1,000 req/min per client. Version-pinned endpoints (v1 stable).

4. Client Dashboard
   React-based web interface. Real-time pipeline monitoring and alerting.
   Role-based access control (admin / analyst / read-only).

5. Data Store
   PostgreSQL (operational data) + S3-compatible object storage (raw file archive).
   Multi-tenant isolation via schema-level separation.

Integration Points:
- Meridian ERP system (inbound data via REST)
- Meridian reporting platform (outbound via webhook)
- NovaBridge internal monitoring stack

Key Design Decisions:
- Stateless processing nodes for horizontal scalability
- End-to-end encryption: TLS 1.3 in transit, AES-256 at rest
- All PII handled under GDPR-compliant data minimization policy

TC-502  JSON ingestion (10k records)                 PASS

TC-504  Date normalization (ISO 8601 input)          PASS

Suite result: 5/7 PASS, 2 FAIL

TC-602  Chart rendering — line chart                 IN PROGRESS

TC-604  Load time benchmark (100k records)           NOT RUN
Suite result: 1/4 complete

=== Open Defects ===
HEL-D-014  Date normalization fails for MM/DD/YYYY input format.
           Affects ~40% of Meridian source data. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Blocked pending Meridian sample data (due 2026-07-15).

HEL-D-015  Large file ingestion (>200k records) causes memory overflow.
           Pipeline crashes at approximately 210k record mark. Severity: HIGH.
           Owner: Amir Hassan. Root cause under investigation.

Decision deadline: 2026-08-01 (landlord requires 5-month notice for non-renewal)

NON-DISCLOSURE AGREEMENT

This Non-Disclosure Agreement ("Agreement") is entered into between
NovaBridge Solutions Inc. ("Disclosing Party") and [VENDOR NAME] ("Receiving Party").

1. Confidential Information
The Receiving Party agrees to hold in strict confidence all technical, business,
and operational information disclosed by the Disclosing Party, including but not
limited to source code, system architecture, client data, and business processes.

2. Term
This Agreement shall remain in effect for three (3) years from the date of signature,
unless terminated earlier by written agreement of both parties.

3. Permitted Disclosures
The Receiving Party may disclose Confidential Information only to employees with a
demonstrated need to know, provided such employees are bound by equivalent obligations.

4. Return of Materials
Upon request or termination, the Receiving Party shall promptly return or certify
destruction of all Confidential Information and copies thereof.

5. Governing Law
This Agreement shall be governed by the laws of the State of Delaware, USA.

IN WITNESS WHEREOF, the parties execute this Agreement as of the date below.

NovaBridge Solutions Inc.
Signature: _________________________ Date: __________
Name / Title:

[VENDOR NAME]
Signature: _________________________ Date: __________

input.content:
Project Helios — Q3 2026 Kickoff Meeting Agenda
Duration: 90 minutes
Facilitator: Sarah Chen (Project Manager)
Technical Lead: Derek Okafor

Attendees: Project Helios core team (PM, Tech Lead, Backend, Frontend, QA, DevOps, Data Eng, UX, BA, Solutions Architect)
Pre-reads (please review before meeting):
  - q3_roadmap_draft.txt
  - project_helios_status.txt
  - meeting_notes_2026_07_08.txt (Meridian sync 7/8)
  - risk_register.txt
  - sprint_14_task_assignments.txt
  - helios_testing_log_sprint14.txt

Meeting Objective:
Align the team on Q3 priorities, milestones, risks, and ownership before Q3 execution begins. Exit with clear owners and dates for July critical path.

---
1. Welcome & Objectives (0:00 - 0:05) | Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Purpose of Q3 kickoff, ground rules, success criteria for meeting

2. Q2 Wrap-Up & Carryover (0:05 - 0:15) | Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor
   - Q2 DONE: Requirements v1.0, architecture design, API gateway, dev env, data schema
   - IN-PROGRESS / OVERDUE from q2_deliverables_checklist.txt:
     * Data ingestion module (85% - target 7/18)
     * Client dashboard v1 (60% - target 7/22)
     * Integration test suite setup (rescheduled to 7/20)
     * UAT environment provisioning (blocked, pending Meridian IT)
     * Performance benchmarking report (overdue)
   - Key learnings from Sprint 14 QA: 10/15 tests executed, 2 HIGH defects open

3. Q3 Roadmap & Key Milestones (0:15 - 0:30) | Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor
   - July: Complete System Integration
     - Data ingestion final (7/18), Dashboard v1 (7/22), Integration test sign-off (7/25)
   - July 25: PRODUCT DEMO FOR MERIDIAN (confirmed)
     - Live ingestion with Meridian sample data, end-to-end walkthrough, dashboard with real data per contract Sec 9.3
   - Aug 17-28: User Acceptance Testing - Meridian Group (1-week extension already consumed)
   - Aug-Sep: Performance Hardening - <2 sec dashboard load @ 100k records, 500k records/min ingestion, external pen test (vendor TBD)
   - September: Launch Prep - Prod env, runbook, 30-day hypercare plan, final client training
   - Target launch: 2026-10-01
   - Critical dependency: UAT slippage >2 weeks pushes launch to Q4 and triggers penalty review per roadmap risk note
   - Proposed Q3 features pending change control: scheduled cron ingestion, email alerting for pipeline failures, self-service mapping rule editor (deferred from Q2)

4. Current Status & Critical Blockers Deep Dive (0:30 - 0:50) | Owner: Derek Okafor
   - Status: ON TRACK (minor risks) per 7/06 status report
     * Ingestion 85%, API Gateway 100%, Dashboard 60%, Integration Testing 40%
   - Sprint 14 highlights (HEL-241 to HEL-248):
     * Blocker HEL-243: Date format normalization - Meridian source has ISO 8601 vs MM/DD/YYYY (~40% affected). Waiting for clean sample dataset from Meridian due 7/15 (Owner: Amir Hassan)
     * Defect HEL-D-014: Date normalization FAIL for MM/DD/YYYY (same root cause)
     * Defect HEL-D-015: Large file ingestion >200k records memory overflow at ~210k mark (Owner: Amir Hassan, under investigation)
     * HEL-246 UAT server provisioning AT RISK (Owner: Nadia Petrov, awaiting IT infra diagram due 7/12, access due 7/19)
     * HEL-247 Performance benchmark not started (Owner: Derek Okafor, dashboard concern raised by Meridian on 7/08)
     * HEL-248 GeoSync v2.1 license renewal coordination (Owner: Raj Patel)
   - Architecture reminder: Ingestion, Transformation (Spark), API Gateway, Dashboard (React), Postgres+S3, stateless nodes, TLS 1.3/AES-256

5. Risks, Dependencies & Mitigation (0:50 - 1:00) | Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Active risks from risk_register.txt:
     * R-01 Data quality (HIGH/HIGH) - mitigated by Meridian sample data
     * R-02 GeoSync license (MEDIUM/HIGH) - legal review due 7/14
     * R-03 Dashboard performance on large datasets (MEDIUM/HIGH) - HEL-247
     * R-04 UAT slippage (HIGH/MED) - extension consumed, need buffer plan
     * R-06 Meridian IT env access delays (MED/MED)
   - Need to discuss cross-training / ownership coverage for key technical areas

6. Meridian Demo Prep - July 25 (1:00 - 1:12) | Owner: Sarah Chen / Derek Okafor / Priya Kapoor sync
   - Meridian commitments: clean sample dataset by 7/15, infra diagram by 7/12, UAT access by 7/19
   - NovaBridge commitments: GeoSync license by 7/14, draft demo agenda to Meridian by 7/18
   - Must be live ingestion, not mock data (per James Whitfield)
   - Action: Assign demo script, data, and environment owners

7. UAT Readiness (Aug 17-28) & Performance / Launch Prep (1:12 - 1:22) | Owner: Kenji Watanabe / Nadia Petrov / Derek Okafor
   - UAT entry criteria, environment readiness, test data, support model for user sessions
   - Feedback triage (budget 2 sprints)
   - Performance hardening: load time target, ingestion benchmark, pen test planning
   - Launch prep: prod setup, runbook, hypercare plan, client training

8. Q3 Ops, Resourcing & Budget Discipline (1:22 - 1:27) | Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Q3 sprint cadence, Q3 planning assumptions
   - Resource allocation vs Q3 roadmap feature additions (change control process)
   - Budget awareness: Q2 overruns noted, need tight cloud cost and vendor license control per finance review (CFO review 7/18, reconciliation plan due 7/20)

9. Wrap-Up, Action Items & Q&A (1:27 - 1:30) | Owner: Sarah Chen
   - Confirm owners, dates, and comms plan
   - Next sync, parking lot items

Expected Outcomes:
- Clear ownership for ingestion finalization, dashboard v1, integration test sign-off, GeoSync renewal, performance benchmark, UAT provisioning
- Agreed demo run-of-show draft and owners by 7/18
- Risk mitigation actions updated in risk_register.txt
- Sprint 15 priorities locked

Notes:
- This is internal agenda. Do not distribute client-confidential data externally.
- Focus discussion on team alignment; HR, finance, and legal details to be handled in separate forums.

content.text:
I've created a 90-minute agenda for the Q3 kickoff and saved it as **q3_kickoff_agenda.txt**.

The agenda is built from the Q3 roadmap draft, current status, Meridian sync notes, risk register, and Sprint 14 assignments:

**Structure (90 min):**
1. **Welcome & Objectives** (0:00-0:05) - Sarah Chen
2. **Q2 Wrap-Up & Carryover** (0:05-0:15) - DONE vs IN-PROGRESS/OVERDUE deliverables, Sprint 14 QA results
3. **Q3 Roadmap & Milestones** (0:15-0:30) - July integration complete, July 25 Meridian Demo (live ingestion required), Aug 17-28 UAT, Aug-Sep performance hardening, Sept launch prep, Oct 1 launch target
4. **Current Status & Critical Blockers** (0:30-0:50) - 85% ingestion / 60% dashboard, HEL-243 date format normalization, HEL-D-014/HEL-D-015 HIGH defects, HEL-246 UAT provisioning at risk, HEL-247 perf benchmark, HEL-248 GeoSync renewal
5. **Risks & Dependencies** (0:50-1:00) - R-01 data quality, R-02 GeoSync license, R-03 dashboard perf, R-04 UAT slippage, R-06 IT access delays
6. **Meridian Demo Prep - July 25** (1:00-1:12) - Meridian commitments (sample data 7/15, infra diagram 7/12) and NovaBridge commitments (license 7/14, demo agenda 7/18)
7. **UAT Readiness & Performance/Launch Prep** (1:12-1:22) - entry criteria, support model, <2s dashboard load, 500k records/min, pen test
8. **Q3 Ops, Resourcing & Budget Discipline** (1:22-1:27) - sprint cadence, change control for new features
9. **Wrap-Up & Next Steps** (1:27-1:30) - owners/dates confirmed

Pre-reads listed in the file: q3_roadmap_draft, project_helios_status, meeting_notes_2026_07_08, risk_register, sprint_14 task assignments, and testing log.
